title: Fix #310: how to guarantee the gate is always clearable (no deadlock)?
context: The deadlock exists because startStage() throws on an out-of-order earlier stage, so once a later stage is recorded (the phantom) planning can never be recorded. Two levers: F2 stop stage-writer recording a code stage before planning/spec (kills the phantom); F3 make startStage record an earlier start instead of throwing (fold still flags real ordering violations non-destructively).

options.label: Adopt BOTH: kill the phantom (F2) AND make startStage tolerant (F3) — strongest guarantee, matches the issue's primary ask
F2 only: kill the phantom, keep startStage's strict out-of-order throw

rationale: Kill the phantom (stage-writer defers recording until planning+spec exist) AND make startStage tolerant of an out-of-order earlier start; fold.computeOrderingViolations remains the sole non-destructive ordering judge.
